items.title: What happened to China’s overseas EV factory boom?
items.summary:
Three charts show China’s promised overseas production hasn’t materialized —
 yet.

items.title: China and the West are taking opposite paths on EV battery recycling
items.summary: China has 85% of the world’s recycling capacity and a mandate to shred old packs. The U.S. has another plan: use them to save the power grid first.

items.title: The AI-powered World Cup runs on thousands of data workers
items.summary: Human annotators in Brazil, Cambodia, and the Philippines are tracking every movement in the football tournament for teams, broadcasters, and the betting industry.

items.title: Chinese universities are cutting language majors to make way for AI
items.summary: Universities are dropping programs in translation and foreign languages while adding degrees in embodied intelligence, AI, and robotics.

items.title: GoPro and Roomba were U.S. pioneers. Chinese rivals now dominate
items.summary: A customer experiences the Osmo Pocket 4 camera at a DJI store in Shanghai, China, on April 20, 2026. (Photo by Costfoto/NurPhoto via Getty Images)

items.title: Chile turned to China for an undersea cable. The U.S. said no
items.summary: A proposed undersea cable from Chile to Hong Kong promised to connect South America directly to Asia. Instead, it became a test of how far the U.S. will go to curb Chinese telecom ambitions.

items.title: When Americans choose Chinese AI
items.summary: Developers say DeepSeek is good enough for a fraction of the cost. “You don’t need God to write your email.”

items.title: Spotify’s post-English AI future
items.summary: More than half of Spotify listening is now in non-English languages as the company expands across Africa, Asia, and Latin America with local artists, pricing, and payment systems.

items.title: Can open-source beat OpenAI?
items.summary: Former Hugging Face executive Tiezhen Wang explains how China's open-source strategy is reshaping the AI race.

items.title: What the SpaceX IPO reveals about Gulf money in AI
items.summary: Saudi Arabia and the UAE are funding America's AI boom — and getting data centers in return.

items.title: China builds a rival satellite constellation as SpaceX goes public
items.summary: A Chinese state-backed satellite company is signing the partners and governments Starlink has pushed aside, days before SpaceX’s record listing.

items.title: Big Tech, big cons: Scammers are hiding in the apps that make your life easy
items.summary: In her new book “Bharat Bluff: Inside the Cons of India’s Internet Revolution,” writer Soumya Gupta explores how fraudsters are exploiting the trust people place in big tech platforms like Google, Facebook, and WhatsApp.
